metadata.title: The insurance / Bermuda-reinsurance endpoint - where the self-marked private credit risk finally lands, and the offshore captive structure that hides it
metadata.frame: The terminal node of the 'who holds the bag' chain (macro-private-credit-marks): PE-owned life insurers fund retiree ANNUITIES with illiquid, manager-marked private credit, then cede the liabilities OFFSHORE to Bermuda/Cayman reinsurers - often CAPTIVES the same group controls, so the 'risk transfer' is largely internal. It compounds every prior theme: self-marked assets, circular (self-dealing) structure, regulatory arbitrage, reduced disclosure - now sitting under ordinary retirement income.
metadata.WARNING: EVIDENCE-GRADED. Scale, flows, and regulator warnings (IMF/FSB/FIO/NAIC/US Treasury) are fact. 'Annuities are at risk' is the regulators' and critics' concern, not a prediction of failure; PE-insurers argue their asset-liability matching is sound. Both stated. Kept out of the proofs.
metadata.method: Bloomberg (America's Insurance / Bermuda series), IMF GFSN + Institutional Investor, FSB (Vulnerabilities in Private Credit, 6 May 2026), NAIC LATF, FIO, US Treasury, Chicago Fed WP, Mayer Brown.
cross_refs: macro-private-credit-marks.json (the asset; this is where it lands)

facts: PE firms control >$700B of US life-insurance assets - ~25% of US life insurers (vs <10% Europe, ~5% Asia). Athene reportedly ~40% of NEW capital entering US life insurance via PE.
Bermuda long-term reinsurance: ~$1.52T assets (Sep 2025), >80% of ceded business originating in the US; US Treasury has joined the scrutiny of this market.
US life/health reinsurance ceded FROM Bermuda entities: ~$928B (2025) vs ~$205B (2014) - a ~4.5x rise in a decade.
In 2025 alone, Apollo's Athene + KKR's Global Atlantic moved >$130B of liabilities to offshore (mainly Bermuda) reinsurers.
~60% of ~$1T US reinsurance purchased in 2024 went OFFSHORE, much of it to CAPTIVES ultimately controlled by the SAME insurer/PE group - so the risk transfer is largely INTERNAL, not genuine.
AM Best: ~1/5 of Athene US Life and KKR Global Atlantic investments are loans to AFFILIATED funds (related-party) - the asset side is self-dealing too (macro-private-credit-marks).
Captive Bermuda/Cayman structures offer lighter capital requirements, alternative accounting, and FEWER disclosures - boosting reported capital ratios without a genuine third party bearing the risk.

Net effect: a US retiree's annuity can be backed by manager-marked private credit (often lent to affiliated funds), with the liability ceded to a captive the same group controls, in a jurisdiction with reduced disclosure - circularity + self-marking + opacity stacked under retirement income.
IMF: raised the alarm on insurers' private-credit exposure and PE influence in life insurance; supervisors urged to coordinate on systemic risk.
FSB: 'Report on Vulnerabilities in Private Credit' (6 May 2026) - private credit ~$1.5-2.0T at end-2024; flags the insurance channel.
FIO (US Federal Insurance Office): warned of PE-led insurers' ties to 'shadow banking' / 'shadow insurance.'
NAIC Life Actuarial Task Force: adopted tougher asset-adequacy / cash-flow-testing guidance (2025) for assets backing business ceded offshore.
Counter-view: PE-insurers (Apollo's Rowan, others) argue long-dated annuity liabilities are well-matched to illiquid long-dated credit and that the model is sound; recorded, not endorsed.

edges.notes: >$130B liabilities (Athene+Global Atlantic) ceded to offshore reinsurers in 2025; ~60% of US reinsurance offshore, much to CAPTIVES the same group controls -> internal (circular) 'risk transfer'.
Retiree annuities backed by manager-marked private credit (often affiliated-fund loans) under reduced-disclosure offshore structures - the terminal bag-holder.
~$180B insurance capital into private credit (2025); the PE owner both originates the credit and owns the insurer that holds it - vertical/circular integration.

assessment: This is the terminal node of the corpus's 'who holds the bag' chain and a compounding of every prior theme. PE firms control >$700B of US life insurance (~25% of the sector), fund retiree annuities with illiquid MANAGER-MARKED private credit (often lent to AFFILIATED funds - self-dealing on the asset side), then cede the liabilities OFFSHORE to Bermuda/Cayman reinsurers - ~$1.52T market, ~$928B ceded from the US (4.5x since 2014), >$130B moved by Athene+Global Atlantic in 2025 alone. The decisive structural fact: ~60% of that reinsurance goes to CAPTIVES the same group controls, so the 'risk transfer' is largely INTERNAL - circular, like the AI loop and the affiliated-fund lending. The result stacks self-marking + circular self-dealing + regulatory arbitrage + reduced disclosure directly under ordinary retirement income, and the official bodies (IMF, FSB, FIO, NAIC, US Treasury) are all now warning on it (PE-insurers' asset-liability-matching defense noted). It does not predict failure - it identifies WHERE the unwind, if it comes, would land: retiree annuities, valued at marks the holders cannot check, in jurisdictions built to disclose less. Overlay; not used in the proofs.
